posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
2008 Vincent Dauvissat (René & Vincent) Chablis 1er Cru Vaillons - France, Burgundy, Chablis, Chablis 1er Cru (2/9/2023)
Takes a while to open up , then shows a light tide pool sea spray nose, lovely medium intensity, beautiful salinity and complexity; more tide pool comes out on the palate, along with wonderfully complex savory/umami minerality. An amalgamation of everything beach: sand, sea, and sun. Minerality, saline, perfectly ripe fruit. Just fabulous, with years ahead of it. (96 points)

link_counts.title: Community Tasting Note - 2008 Vincent Dauvissat (René & Vincent) Chablis 1er Cru Vaillons - CellarTracker

posts.name: Andy K0b3rl
posts.cooked: A 96 from you? From what I can see, you only rated Gonon VV and Juge higher… I need to drink more 2008 Dauvissat

posts.name: Ryan Caughey
posts.cooked: Amazing. My first bottle of 08 Dauvissat Clos was perhaps the finest young Chablis I’ve ever had. My remaining to were completely premox.

posts.name: Kent Comley
posts.cooked: Bit jelly here…thanks for the thrill. When they’re on, they’re on!

link_counts.title: TN: 2007 Vincent Dauvissat (René & Vincent) Chablis 1er Cru La Forest

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked: I keep, hearing that. So I think, I should sell some, particularly for the market prices today. Then I drink one, and decide I can’t sell something this good.

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked: How bad would people here asses the premox risk to be with Dauvissat? Mostly academic to me, but I have a bottle of the 2016 La Forest in the cellar and the CT notes are all over the place, ranging from premox to decent to relatively lavish praise.

posts.name: Larry Link
posts.cooked:
I’ve had a disappointing 20% premox rate on Dauvissat, in particular 2008 to 2013 has been particularly bad. Most recently a 2008 Les Clos and a 2009 La Forest were both premoxed.

When they are on, they rival Raveneau, but I always feel like I’m playing Russian Roulette when I open one.

posts.name: Ilkka |_ |_| mme
posts.cooked:
Larry_Link:

I’ve had a disappointing 20% premox rate on Dauvissat, in particular 2008 to 2013 has been particularly bad. Most recently a 2008 Les Clos and a 2009 La Forest were both premoxed.

When they are on, they rival Raveneau, but I always feel like I’m playing Russian Roulette when I open one.

Damn, that’s not great. I’ve only had the village Chablis and several vintages of La Forest and all have been sound and good but not really Raveneau level for my palate.

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
Larry_Link:

When they are on, they rival Raveneau

When they are on, they surpass Raveneau!
I have opened 70 bottles of 2008 across all the vineyards and have only had 3 premoxed bottles. Luck I guess.

posts.name: Stuart BeauneHead Niemtzow
posts.cooked:
This is the last vintage of Dauvissat in my collection, sadly. The good news is that there’s a fair amount left of it. Had a similar experience with the '08 Vaillons in 2011,

*/11: exuberant acidity; make sure to serve warm enough. Although the acidity prevails , the finish is med long and fruity…so, it’s well balanced though a little on the lean side. Give this 15 yerars…to 2023 or beyond. Good food wine and actually quite delicious. 3/ 3-…

posts.name: Stuart BeauneHead Niemtzow
posts.cooked: Well…it’s hard to think of a more consistently good WB producer over the years…than Dauvissat.

posts.name: Larry Link
posts.cooked:
Stuart_BeauneHead_Niemtzow:

Well…it’s hard to think of a more consistently good WB producer over the years…than Dauvissat.

That may be your experience but it’s not mine. PYCM has been the most consistently good, followed in order by Raveneau, Coche, Roulot and then a distant last Dauvissat. Over the last 8 years my CellarTracker stats show 697 bottles of white burgundy consumed from the 5 producers mentioned with the following flawed/oxidized rates by producer:

Producer. Flawed/Total Bottles % flawed
PYCM 3/169, 1.8% flawed
Raveneau 5/191, 2.6% flawed
Coche 2/78, 2.6% flawed
Roulot 8/147, 5.4% flawed
Dauvissat 21/112, 18.8% flawed

posts.name: alan weinberg
posts.cooked:
Stuart_BeauneHead_Niemtzow:

Well…it’s hard to think of a more consistently good WB producer over the years…than Dauvissat

welcome back!

posts.name: Joel Deutsch
posts.cooked: I bought 6 of the Dauvissat Chablis “La Forest.” I’ve had two or three, every one of them oxidized.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: And yet they are apparently thinking about moving to DIAM all the same…

posts.name: Alan Rath
posts.cooked:
I’m all for that!

I have yet to open a diam closed Fevre that was premoxed.

But then I’d love it if every wine came in a screwcap Bordeaux bottle lol.

posts.name: Robert Grenley
posts.cooked: Well, I am happy to hear that they are finally moving (or finally considering moving) through the denial stage, which they have reportedly been stuck in for some time, and into the acceptance stage. Regardless of how well their wines have been reviewed upon release, their recent price escalations made their wines less alluring when considering their premox risk.
